items.text: He wasn't just seeing the grid. The grid was seeing him with something like triangle eyes, it was mapping him. Cataloging every cell. Taking samples from his optic nerve, his retina, the soft tissue behind his eyes where memory lived.

items.text: The room filled with a phosphorescent glow. The cat seemed to vibrate under his hand. A humming sound traveled up his arm and rattled his teeth. And for a moment, the cat was not a cat. items.text: Eighty trunks left the Hotel New Yorker. Only sixty arrived at the museum in Belgrade years later. Twenty trunks vanished. One quarter of his life's work. Gone. Vaporized into the dark machinery of government. Along with a small ledger that no inventory ever recorded.

items.text: If you carry the residue of the old world, if the original pattern still pulses somewhere beneath the sealed skin, you see red. A deep, earthy, wine-dark red. Maroon. Burgundy. The color of velvet curtains in an old theater. The color of dried roses pressed between the pages of a book.
items.title: Ep2 - lore_fact

items.text: It wasn't radiant heat. It didn't warm his skin. It burned his shirt off his back.

items.text: Four thousand miles to the east, hours had passed. The white sun had crossed its zenith and begun its slow descent toward the Velebit mountains. The village of Smiljan had emerged from its terror???doors opening, voices calling across yards, the rhythm of ordinary life reasserting itself over the memory of the red sky.
items.title: Ep2 - lore_fact

items.text: In Boston, George Bliss watched the sparks stop flying from his telegraph.
items.title: Ep2 - lore_fact

items.text: Bliss pushed himself up. The hair on his arms rose. The air around the desk had weight. It pushed back against him???not wind, not pressure, but resistance. The atmosphere had thickened into something that didn't want to be walked through.
items.title: Ep2 - character_state
